Title: The Poisonwood Bible
Author: Barbara Kingsolver
Personal_Notes: Reread. Congo missions and faith unraveling. Hit differently this time after ICU work.

Title: Prodigal Summer
Author: Barbara Kingsolver
Personal_Notes: Beautiful and grounding. Read it slowly through December.

Title: The Gift of Being Yourself
Author: David Benner
Personal_Notes: Gave me language for a struggle I'd been carrying. Dog-eared half the pages.

Title: Braving the Wilderness
Author: Brene Brown
Personal_Notes: Solid. Not her strongest but the chapter on collective pain was worth the book.

Title: The Interior Castle
Author: Teresa of Avila
Personal_Notes: Slow and rich. Read alongside morning pages. Transformative.

Title: A Gentleman in Moscow
Author: Amor Towles
Personal_Notes: Pure joy to read. Borrowed from Midtown Pages.

Title: The Return of the Prodigal Son
Author: Henri Nouwen
Personal_Notes: Third read. The father figure. Always find something new.

Title: Abide with Me
Author: Elizabeth Strout
Personal_Notes: A pastor's grief. Read slowly. Some pages I had to set down.

Title: Just Mercy
Author: Bryan Stevenson
Personal_Notes: Wept twice. Shared chapter four with Naomi.

Title: A Ladder to the Sky
Author: John Boyne
Personal_Notes: Compelling but deeply uncomfortable protagonist. Talked it through with Sandra.

Title: Searching for Sunday
Author: Rachel Held Evans
Personal_Notes: This woman. Read it in three days. Ordered two copies as gifts.

Title: Their Eyes Were Watching God
Author: Zora Neale Hurston
Personal_Notes: For the third time. Never gets old. Janie's voice stays with you.

Title: The Road Back to You
Author: Ian Morgan Cron
Personal_Notes: Enneagram for church folks. I am a 2 with a very loud 1 wing.

Title: Educated
Author: Tara Westover
Personal_Notes: Couldn't stop. The education and family dynamics. Thought of Marcus reading it.

Title: Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil
Author: John Berendt
Personal_Notes: Sandra's recommendation. Savannah Gothic. Enjoyed it more than expected.

Title: Jesus and the Disinherited
Author: Howard Thurman
Personal_Notes: Essential. This book should be required reading everywhere.

Title: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings
Author: Maya Angelou
Personal_Notes: Another annual reread. Angelou never fails.

Title: The Unsettled
Author: Ayana Mathis
Personal_Notes: Memphis setting made it personal. The migration narrative hit home.

Title: Walking with God through Pain and Suffering
Author: Timothy Keller
Personal_Notes: Read in parallel with a difficult stretch on the palliative floor.

Title: Homegoing
Author: Yaa Gyasi
Personal_Notes: One of the best novels I've read. Shared it with Naomi.

Title: Becoming
Author: Michelle Obama
Personal_Notes: Picked it back up. Obama's arc of identity feels relevant every reading.

Title: Listening Below the Noise
Author: Anne LeClaire
Personal_Notes: Short. About silence. Good for this season.

Title: Anne of Green Gables
Author: L.M. Montgomery
Personal_Notes: Read this with Naomi over her birthday visit. Pure comfort.

Title: The Book of Ruth
Author: Jane Hamilton
Personal_Notes: Rural Midwest darkness. Stayed with me.

Title: The Healing
Author: Jonathan Odell
Personal_Notes: Memphis historical novel, slavery and faith. Devastating and necessary.

Title: The Night Gospel
Author: Mark Scandrette
Personal_Notes: Holiday reading. Quiet and good.

Title: Demon Copperhead
Author: Barbara Kingsolver
Personal_Notes: Her Pulitzer. Dickens retelling in Appalachia. Magnificent.

Title: The Art of Losing
Author: Kevin Young
Personal_Notes: Grief poetry. Read alongside a difficult chaplaincy week.
